title: centaurea attica subsp. pateraea
intro: Centaurea attica subsp. pateraea, commonly known as Attica knapweed or mountain centaury, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family native to Greece and Turkey. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral effects, which are attributed to its essential oils and other bioactive compounds. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including the reduction of fever, relief from pain and inflammation, and the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Centaurea attica subsp. pateraea is typically prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
